description_long: This is a modular shelving unit composed of four distinct vertical sections, each featuring multiple horizontal shelves. The structure is primarily made of metal, with thin, tubular supports and flat, rectangular shelves that appear to be either metal or coated wood. The shelves are open on all sides, allowing for easy access and visibility of stored items, and the design is minimalist, with no visible doors or enclosed compartments. The unit is likely intended for use in garages, workshops, or urban storage spaces, providing ample surface area for organizing tools, supplies, or other items. The shelves are evenly spaced, with some sections featuring slightly deeper or wider shelves for larger objects. The overall appearance is utilitarian and robust, suitable for environments where durability and accessibility are prioritized.
description: A modular, open-frame shelving unit with four vertical sections and multiple flat shelves, made primarily of metal. Its minimalist, utilitarian design is ideal for urban storage or garage use.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four shelving sections, with open shelves and no doors or handles visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the unit and the alignment of the shelves, emphasizing the open, airy structure.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the open backs of the shelves and the supporting frame.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the slim, vertical supports and the depth of the shelves.
